title: UN Security Council Remains Divided on Iran Nuclear Path
detail: UN Under-Secretary-General Rosemary DiCarlo briefs the Security Council on JCPOA implementation, reporting no agreement on the way forward regarding Iran's nuclear programme despite intensified diplomacy in the second half of 2025. Iran's Ambassador Amir-Saeid Iravani insists Resolution 2231 expired on October 18 and 'ceased to have any legal effect.' Western members defend the snapback sanctions triggered in September, while Russia and China call the mechanism illegitimate.

media.caption: UN Security Council members debate Iran nuclear path amid JCPOA deadlock

title: Satellite Imagery Shows Concealment Work at Natanz
detail: Planet Labs satellite imagery from December 3 reveals that damage at Iran's Natanz Pilot Fuel Enrichment Plant has been covered by a white roofing structure, obscuring the extent of reconstruction from overhead observation. Analysts note that two tunnel entrances near Isfahan have been packed with dirt as a protective measure against potential future strikes. The IAEA, blocked from on-site inspections since Iran withdrew from the Cairo agreement in November, relies on satellite imagery as its only monitoring tool.

media.caption: Satellite images show concealment structures placed over damaged Natanz nuclear facility

title: Houthis Release 11 Crew Members from Sunken Cargo Ship
detail: Yemen's Houthi rebels release 11 crew members of the Greek-owned Eternity C cargo vessel, which was attacked and sunk in July 2025 near Al-Hodeidah. The release, brokered through Omani mediation, sees the crew flown from Sana'a to Muscat. Nine of the freed sailors are Filipino nationals. The move is seen as a goodwill gesture during the Houthis' pause in Red Sea attacks following the October Gaza ceasefire, though the group warns operations will resume if Israel breaks the truce.

media.caption: Crew members of the sunken cargo ship Eternity C released by Houthi rebels after months of captivity
